approach: Pure-Python stdlib xlsx parser (zipfile + xml.etree.ElementTree). No openpyxl, no com.crealytics.spark.excel jar. Works in PyPI-isolated AIDP clusters.
test_path: Reconstituted a 5,460-byte .xlsx from base64-embedded content, wrote to a Volume, parsed via stdlib zipfile + xml.etree (sharedStrings.xml + worksheets/sheet1.xml), built a Spark DataFrame from the parsed rows, persisted as CSV to the Volume, and re-read via Spark CSV. Demonstrates Excel ingestion in AIDP clusters with NO non-stdlib Python deps and NO Java JARs.
fix_origin: v0.3.0 — stdlib xlsx parser added to `oracle_ai_data_platform_connectors.excel.read_xlsx_stdlib()` and skill SKILL.md updated with this as Option C (works when openpyxl is unavailable AND com.crealytics jar is unavailable).
